meaning: có thể phân hủy sinh học
description_en: Capable of being decomposed by bacteria or other living organisms.
example: Always try to use biodegradable packaging to reduce waste.

description_en: The action of conserving something, in particular preservation, protection, or restoration of the natural environment.
example: Wildlife conservation is essential for maintaining biodiversity.
note: Includes forest, water, and animal protection.

description_en: The action of clearing a wide area of trees.
example: Deforestation in the Amazon is a major contributor to global warming.

description_en: A biological community of interacting organisms and their physical environment.
example: The coral reef is a complex and fragile ecosystem.

description_en: The production and discharge of something, especially gas or radiation.
example: The new law aims to reduce carbon emissions from factories.
note: Often associated with air pollution and climate change.

description_en: Relating to or produced by the internal heat of the earth.
example: Iceland uses geothermal energy to heat Most of its homes.

description_en: The natural home or environment of an animal, plant, or other organism.
example: The polar bear's natural habitat is disappearing due to melting ice.
note: Specific area where a species thrives.

description_en: The action of insulating something or the state of being insulated.
example: Good attic insulation can significantly reduce heating bills.

description_en: The disposal of waste material by burying it, especially as a method of filling in or extending usable land.
example: Too Much plastic ends up in landfills every year.

description_en: The presence in or introduction into the environment of a substance that has harmful or poisonous effects.
example: Air pollution is a major problem in many industrial cities.
note: Can affect air, water, or soil.

description_en: Not depleted when used.
example: Solar and wind are popular forms of renewable energy.

description_en: The ability to be maintained at a certain rate or level.
example: Sustainability is at the core of our business model.
note: Meeting current needs without compromising future generations.

description_en: Poisonous.
example: Chemical runoff from farms can be toxic to fish.

description_en: Wild animals collectively; the native fauna of a region.
example: The national park is home to a wide variety of wildlife.

description_en: A layer in the earth's stratosphere that absorbs Most of the sun's ultraviolet radiation.
example: The ozone layer protects us from harmful UV rays.
note: Damage here leads to increased UV exposure.

description_en: Convert waste into reusable material.
example: We should all try to recycle paper, plastic, and glass.
note: One of the 3 Rs (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle).

description_en: A body of permeable rock which can contain or transmit groundwater.
example: Many rural communities depend on aquifers for their drinking water.

description_en: The variety of plant and animal life in the world or in a particular habitat.
example: Tropical rainforests have incredibly high biodiversity.

description_en: Involving or causing sudden great damage or suffering.
example: The oil spill had catastrophic effects on the local marine life.

description_en: The action or state of making or being made impure by polluting or poisoning.
example: The factory was closed due to soil contamination.

description_en: A prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall, leading to a shortage of water.
example: The severe drought led to widespread crop failure.
note: Can last for months or years.

description_en: The branch of biology that deals with the relations of organisms to one another and to their physical surroundings.
example: She is studying the ecology of the Great Barrier Reef.

description_en: The process of eroding or being eroded by wind, water, or other natural agents.
example: Beach erosion is a major problem for coastal cities.

description_en: A chemical or natural substance added to soil or land to increase its fertility.
example: Excessive use of chemical fertilizers can pollute groundwater.

description_en: A slowly moving mass or river of ice formed by the accumulation and compaction of snow on mountains or near the poles.
example: Glaciers are melting at an alarming rate due to rising global temperatures.

description_en: Risky; dangerous.
example: Chemical waste must be disposed of in a specialized hazardous waste facility.

description_en: The supply of water to land or crops to help growth, typically by means of channels.
example: Efficient irrigation systems can save a lot of water.

description_en: All the visible features of an area of countryside or land.
example: The volcanic landscape of the island is quite unique.

description_en: The branch of science concerned with the processes and phenomena of the atmosphere, especially as a means of forecasting the weather.
example: Advances in meteorology have improved our ability to predict hurricanes.

description_en: Materials or substances such as minerals, forests, water, and fertile land that occur in nature and can be used for economic gain.
example: Countries should use their natural resources responsibly.

description_en: Produced or involving production without the use of chemical fertilizers, pesticides, or other artificial agents.
example: Organic farming is better for the soil and local wildlife.

description_en: A substance used for destroying insects or other organisms harmful to cultivated plants or to animals.
example: The use of pesticides has fallen significantly as farmers switch to organic methods.
note: Can have harmful ecological side effects.

description_en: A place, typically a large, deep pit, from which stone or other materials are or have been extracted.
example: The old limestone quarry has been turned into a nature reserve.

description_en: Sending out light; shining or glowing brightly.
example: The sun is a source of radiant energy.
note: Usually refers to light or heat energy.

meaning: khu bảo tồn; nơi trú ẩn
description_en: A place of refuge or safety.
example: The island is a sanctuary for endangered sea birds.

description_en: A river or stream flowing into a larger river or lake.
example: The Missouri River is a major tributary of the Mississippi.

description_en: Shrubs and small trees forming the undergrowth in a forest.
example: The deer disappeared into the thick underbrush.

description_en: The provision of fresh air to a room or building.
example: Good ventilation is important to maintain indoor air quality.

description_en: Land consisting of marshes or swamps; saturated land.
example: Wetlands are critical habitats for many migratory birds.

description_en: Feeding on or boring into wood.
example: Termites are xylophagous insects that can damage houses.

description_en: Produce or provide an agricultural or industrial product.
example: The crop yield was lower than usual this year due to the lack of rain.

description_en: The scientific study of the behavior, structure, physiology, classification, and distribution of animals.
example: He decided to major in zoology because of his love for animals.

description_en: Relating to the atmosphere of the earth or another planet.
example: The increase in atmospheric CO2 is a major concern.

description_en: The amount of carbon dioxide and other carbon compounds emitted due to the consumption of fossil fuels by a particular person, group, etc.
example: Flying less is one of the best ways to reduce your carbon footprint.

description_en: Capable of being broken down into simpler parts.
example: Most food waste is readily decomposable.

description_en: At serious risk of extinction.
example: The giant panda is no longer an endangered species, but it is still vulnerable.
note: Species at high risk of disappearing.

description_en: A natural fuel such as coal or gas, formed in the geological past from the remains of living organisms.
example: We need to transition away from fossil fuels to renewable energy.

description_en: The science that deals with the earth's physical structure and substance.
example: The geology of the region explains why it is prone to earthquakes.
note: Study of earth's history and rocks.

description_en: The process or period of gathering in crops.
example: The grape harvest begins in September.

description_en: Water that has percolated through a solid and leached out some of the constituents.
example: Landfills must have liners to prevent leachate from contaminating groundwater.
